title: Reverse LL in group of given size K

content.problemStatement:
Given the head of a singly linked list containing integers, reverse the nodes of the list in groups of k and return the head of the modified list. If the number of nodes is not a multiple of k, then the remaining nodes at the end should be kept as is and not reversed.




Do not change the values of the nodes, only change the links between nodes.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain the head of a singly linked list and an integer k. The head will be represented as a list of integers, and k will be a single integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the head of the modified linked list, represented as a list of integers.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a two-pointer approach. We will keep track of the previous group's tail and the current group's head. We will then reverse the current group and connect it to the previous group.

code.Python3:
# Definition for singly-linked list node
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.next = None

class Solution:
    # Function to reverse nodes in k-sized groups
    def reverseKGroup(self, head, k):
        # Creating a dummy node
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head

        # Pointer to the tail of the last reversed group
        groupPrev = dummy

        while True:
            # Get the k-th node in the group
            kth = self.getKthNode(groupPrev, k)
            if not kth:
                break

            # Store the next group’s start
            groupNext = kth.next

            # Reverse the current k nodes
            prev = groupNext
            curr = groupPrev.next

            for _ in range(k):
                temp = curr.next
                curr.next = prev
                prev = curr
                curr = temp

            # Connect the reversed group to the previous part
            temp = groupPrev.next
            groupPrev.next = kth
            groupPrev = temp

        # Return new head
        return dummy.next

    # Helper function to find the k-th node from current
    def getKthNode(self, curr, k):
        while curr and k > 0:
            curr = curr.next
            k -= 1
        return curr
